extra.reasoning:
The property is monotonicity in back_pain: flipping back_pain from false to true (all other inputs fixed) must never lower acuity.

Examining the rule set, back_pain does not appear in any rule condition, nor in the default. The six rules fire only on leg_swelling, chest_pain, severe_headache, pallor, dyspnea, and confusion. The default acuity := routine applies only when no rule fires, and that determination also does not depend on back_pain.

Therefore acuity is functionally independent of back_pain: for any fixed assignment of the other six inputs, the set of matching rules, the priority/order conflict resolution, and the default-application decision are all identical whether back_pain is true or false. The resulting acuity value is exactly equal in both cases.

Since "equal" satisfies the requirement "no lower than before," there is no input for which setting back_pain true lowers acuity. The property holds across the entire input space (all 2^7 = 128 input combinations).
